methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $369M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Augury, a Series F company in Robotics & Manufacturing founded by Gal Shaul and Saar Yoskovitz, has raised $369M to date. The valuation landscape for this stage in the sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and seven private peers, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier was not available for reliable median-based triangulation.

Given the limited peer comparability data, the stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($369M) as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F to later-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $480M. This method reflects the magnitude of equity deployed and expected value creation momentum at this stage, though it does not incorporate company-specific revenue, profitability, or capital efficiency metrics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $408M to $552M, centered on $480M. The median estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single methodological pillar and the absence of tight peer comparables in robotics and manufacturing at comparable scale and stage. Key unknowns affecting this range include Augury's revenue run-rate, unit economics, and path to cash-flow breakeven relative to peer profiles. Further refinement would require either tighter same-stage comparable data or access to financial performance metrics.
